architecture.key_finding: NEW-ARCH-C-1: single rollbackable transaction promise impossible with internally-committing repo methods; NEW-ARCH-H-1: after-commit dispatch cannot be rolled back.

completeness.key_finding: COMP-H-013: US-9 still requires localized response body on a 204/no-body path (FR-020/SC-026).

key_findings: C3-001: event dispatch both after-commit AND transaction-rollback protected — impossible
C3-002: US-9 requires response body on 204 no-meal-plan path
C3-003: US-9/event-payload localization surface underspecified (no message_key on payload)
C3-004: Out-of-scope locale text regressed to single-locale claim
EXEC-C-001: single transaction impossible reusing add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (internal commits)
EXEC-C-002: event exactly-once + rollback impossible with after-commit dispatch
EXEC-H-001: run-now 204 contract still contradicts US-9
EXEC-M-001: locale OOS text contradicts corrected locale policy
EXEC-M-002: PostgreSQL default isolation level wording wrong (REPEATABLE READ -> READ COMMITTED)
EXEC-M-003: FR-014/FR-023 should cite filter implementation lines

convergent_root_cause: Six of seven critical+high findings across all 4 axes converge on a single architectural impossibility: v3 promised that a single DB transaction could rollback CAS marker + shopping-list writes + post-commit event dispatch. This is impossible because (a) RepositoryGeneric.create_many/update_many/update call session.commit() internally (mealie/repos/repository_generic.py:195-208, :210-226, :228-244); (b) bulk_create_items delegates to those committing methods; (c) EventBusService.dispatch publishes immediately to external sinks. A reviewer-recommended outbox pattern + no-commit refactor cleanly resolves all four overlapping findings.

actions.action: Escalate the transactional architecture decision as NC-004 with recommended_default=outbox pattern + no-commit refactor; if_rejected=partial-failure tolerance with sync_attempt_id idempotency. Add FR-030 (commit:bool=True kwarg on RepositoryGeneric.create_many/update_many/update + ShoppingListService.bulk_create_items/add_recipe_ingredients_to_list — guards the internal session.commit() calls). Add FR-031 (event_outbox table + dispatch_event_outbox minutely scheduler task + RepositoryEventOutbox). Rewrite FR-011 to use the outbox pattern: open outer `with session.begin():`, CAS UPDATE, bulk_create_items(commit=False), INSERT event_outbox row, single atomic commit. Rewrite FR-012 to clarify outbox-aware rollback semantics. Rewrite FR-020 to mirror the outbox pattern in force-mode. Update FR-024 step D to create event_outbox table + indices. Update SC-007/SC-013/SC-025 to reference outbox row counts instead of dispatch counts. Add SC-030 (commit=False suppresses internal commit), SC-031 (dispatcher poll + retry behavior), SC-032 (atomic 3-stage rollback).

Rewrite US-9 description / independent_test / AC1 / AC2 / AC3 to align with the FR-020 / SC-026 204-no-body contract: precondition-failure response is 204 with zero body bytes; i18n key surfaces in (a) server-side WARN logs and (b) the new optional `message_key` field on EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData (FR-021).

Add optional `message_key: str | None = None` field to EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData (FR-021). The outbox dispatcher (FR-031) forwards it into EventBusService.dispatch(..., message=message_key or '') so Apprise/webhook subscribers receive the key via EventBusMessage.body. Update FR-022 to explicitly state the i18n keys surface in (a) logs and (b) message_key field — NOT in HTTP response body.

Replace the stale Out-of-Scope sentence 'Mealie currently ships only en-US.json' with 'Mealie ships 40+ Crowdin-managed locale files at mealie/lang/messages/*.json; per .github/copilot-instructions.md Translations section only en-US.json is editable; PRs touching other locales are rejected.' This aligns with FR-022 + Assumption #3.

Fix the two-replica edge case wording: PostgreSQL default isolation level is READ COMMITTED (not REPEATABLE READ); add note that READ COMMITTED still serializes concurrent UPDATEs on the same row via row-level write locks. SQLite's per-statement lock wording is retained.

Add citations to mealie/repos/repository_generic.py for the actual filter implementation: _filter_builder L94-102, get_one L156-179, page_all L315-355 — on FR-014 and FR-023 — so the 'WHERE clause on every query' claim is concretely verifiable.

Add reciprocal SC->FR links: FR-020.related_success_criteria += SC-026; FR-022.related_success_criteria += SC-026; FR-021.related_success_criteria += SC-027; FR-024.related_success_criteria += SC-027; FR-001.related_success_criteria += SC-028; FR-023.related_success_criteria += SC-029.
